description: An instrumentation engineer needs to configure combustible gas detectors for a chemical plant. Calculate the 'Low Alarm' setpoints for Methane, Propane, Hydrogen, and Acetone using data from CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/). For each chemical, identify the Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) in percent volume (using NIOSH values) and calculate the safety setpoint as 10% of that LEL. Create a pipe-delimited report at /home/ga/Documents/alarm_setpoints.txt with the format 'Chemical Name | LEL (%) | Setpoint (ppm)', converting the final setpoint to parts per million (ppm) where 1% volume equals 10,000 ppm.
